sections.paragraph:
ἀποπαύω 1 aor. ἀπέπαυσα (s. παύω; Hom., Hdt. et al.; Ps 88:45 Sym.; ApcMos 6; Jos., Ant. 7, 308 [Naber], also s. Jos. Lex. s.v. on 6, 117; ApcMos 6; Tat. 17, 1) to make to cease from an activity, put to rest. After nursing Mary, Anna ἀπέπαυσεν αὐτὴν ἐν τῷ κοιτῶνι put her to rest in the bedroom GJs 6:3 Bodm. (ἀνέπαυσεν Strycker, who suggests that the copyist of Bodmer mistook ν for π (s. ἀναπαύω); v.l. ἀνεκλινεν.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Jos. Jos. Lex. = A Lexicon to Josephus, incomplete—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

rest. rest. = restored, restoration

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Bodm Bodm = Bodmer Papyri—List 4

Strycker Strycker = ÉdeStrycker, s. GJs in List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 118
